title: Astronauts Return Safely from a Trip Around the Moon
summary: Four astronauts came back to Earth after a daring trip around the Moon. They flew in a spacecraft called Orion, which landed in the Pacific Ocean near San Diego. The astronauts—Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen—were picked up by a Navy ship and are all healthy and happy. This mission, called Artemis II, was the first time people have gone to the Moon in over 50 years. They didn't land on the Moon but took amazing pictures and set a record for being the farthest humans have ever traveled from Earth. NASA plans to send more missions to land on the Moon soon.

background_read: The Moon is Earth's closest neighbor in space, and people first landed there in 1969. NASA is the U.S. space agency that sends astronauts on missions. The Artemis program aims to return humans to the Moon and explore it more. This mission was special because it was the first crewed trip to the Moon in over 50 years, showing how space exploration is continuing with new technology and teamwork.
Article_Structure: WHO: Four astronauts—Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen—and NASA.
WHAT: They completed a mission called Artemis II, flying around the Moon and returning safely to Earth.
WHERE: They launched from Florida, traveled to the Moon, and landed in the Pacific Ocean near San Diego.
WHY: To test new spacecraft and prepare for future missions to land on the Moon and explore space.

perspectives.description: This mission shows human curiosity and the desire to explore new places in space.
